note.soap:
(NOTE: LLM note generation unavailable: NameError: name 'k' is not defined)
S: 34-year-old patient reports a tick bite after exposure in Georgia with the tick attached for approximately 37 hours. Patient describes a rash with a bullseye / erythema migrans appearance. Patient is not pregnant.
O: No physical exam available due to phone-only encounter. Patient describes a characteristic erythema migrans-type rash (bullseye appearance).
A: Phone-history consistent with tick bite; consider early Lyme disease if erythema migrans is present.
P:
- Disposition: Outpatient management with clinician review for antibiotic selection when indicated.
- Plan: If erythema migrans suspected, recommended regimen (reviewing physician to confirm/prescribe): doxycycline 100 mg PO BID x 10 days (adult). Alternatives: amoxicillin 500 mg PO TID x 14 days OR cefuroxime axetil 500 mg PO BID x 14 days.
- Safety net: Seek urgent care/ED for fever, severe headache/neck stiffness, neurologic symptoms, spreading rash, or worsening illness.
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
Medication options you may consider if safe (no dosing by phone): acetaminophen (Tylenol) if you can take it safely; ibuprofen (Advil) if you can take it safely.
